purpose: Authoritative schema-rules contract for VIGIL as a public evidence-to-repair observatory for observations, failure modes, proposals, and implemented patch notes. Source evidence remains embedded in the relevant record; ecosystem persistence, CAM repair, and implementation verification are represented separately. Interpretive provenance identifies the AI analytical reviewer, human governance editor, capability profile, source modality, primary-artefact access, and review limitations. Interpretive provenance identifies the AI analytical reviewer, human governance editor, capability profile, source modality, primary-artefact access, and review limitations. Interpretive provenance identifies the AI analytical reviewer, human governance editor, capability profile, source modality, primary-artefact access, and review limitations. Interpretive provenance identifies the AI analytical reviewer, human governance editor, capability profile, source modality, primary-artefact access, and review limitations.

individual_records: source_records is the only canonical source-evidence block in individual records.
source_data is forbidden in individual records.
source_data.sources is forbidden in individual records.
linked_records.external_references must not duplicate the primary evidence source already held in source_records.
Source evidence belongs in source_records within the substantive record it evidences; it is not a separate VIGIL record class.
An OBS record must preserve a material unresolved governance proposition and must not merely duplicate evidence for an existing FM, PROP, or PATCH.
Internal curator instructions such as 'add this incident to' or 'do not amend in this pass' are forbidden in public record content.
Each source record must state evidence_modality.
Each source record must state primary_artefact_access, including whether direct primary review occurred.
A transcript, screenshot, summary, or human description must not be represented as equivalent to direct audiovisual or interaction review.
Each source record must state evidence_modality.
Each source record must state primary_artefact_access, including whether direct primary review occurred.
A transcript, screenshot, summary, or human description must not be represented as equivalent to direct audiovisual or interaction review.

Each source record must state primary_artefact_access, including whether direct primary review occurred.

A transcript, screenshot, summary, or human description must not be represented as equivalent to direct audiovisual or interaction review.
generated_registers: Generated registers may contain derived source_summary fields.
source_summary is derived from source_records and is not maintained by individual record files.
canonical_source_url_status_values: available / canonical main-branch confirmation pending exact line review
VIGIL local / pending public record URL if applicable
available / pending archive and evidence verification

observation.description: Material unresolved governance observation / early warning record; not a duplicate source-evidence container.
observation.cam_routing: Use related_or_similar_* fields only. OBS preserves a substantive unresolved governance proposition and uncertainty; source evidence for an existing record belongs in that record's source_records.

failure_mode.cam_routing: Use affected_* fields for triage-relevant CAM routing.
failure_mode.repair_status_interpretation: repair_status describes the CAM-side governance response only. It must not be read as proof that an external ecosystem failure has ceased.

proposal.cam_routing: Use target_* fields. A PROP must not claim implementation as a completed patch.

patch.cam_routing: Use changed_* fields. PATCH records require implemented-change evidence.

enum: civil society safety assessment / public-interest report

system_context_rules.description: system_context describes the observed/proposed system, platform, product, service, runtime, interface, deployment, or governance corpus using normalised field names.
system_context_rules.specific_model_or_runtime_rule: Non-empty string; intentionally not an enum because runtime names and tool-augmented modes drift frequently.
system_context_rules.interface_surface_rule: Non-empty string or array of non-empty strings describing where or how behaviour appears.

cam_internal_rules.description: cam_internal describes CAM routing, targets, affected areas, or changed components.

cam_failure_taxonomy.taxonomy_reference: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
cam_failure_taxonomy.derived_from: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy, including §3.11 Economic & Legitimacy Failures as economic-legitimacy.

generated_register_rules.pruning: Generated index manifests prune empty arrays, empty strings, empty objects, and nulls while preserving meaningful uncertainty values.
generated_register_rules.index_entry_contract: Generated type indexes and the master registry contain lean collapsed-list metadata plus canonical record location fields only. Detailed summaries and full evidence/context structures remain in individual canonical record JSON files.
type_specific_list_fields: failure severity, likelihood, classification, and triage filter fields
patch/change type/status and verification filter fields

linked_records_rules.standards: linked_records.standards is reserved for external standards, laws, regulatory instruments, technical standards, and external governance references. CAM instrument IDs such as CAM-BS* and CAM-EQ* and VIGIL record IDs such as VIGIL-* belong in cam_internal routing fields, using related/similar for observations, affected for failure modes, target for proposals, and changed for patches.

record_state_rules.notes: Failure modes remain active when repaired; repair progress is represented through triage.mitigation_status, linked_records.related_patch_notes, and optional repair_status.

runtime_conformance_rules.description: Optional runtime-bounded evidence objects distinguish an implemented CAM repair from observed conformance or non-conformance in named vendor, platform, runtime, model, client, or interface formations. Runtime names remain open-ended and cross-runtime transfer is never presumed.

runtime_conformance.count_rule: Counts must be non-negative integers and must equal the corresponding detail-array length whenever that array is present.
runtime_conformance.runtime_identifier_rule: vendor, platform, runtime, model, and client values are open-ended non-empty strings; no vendor or runtime names are hard-coded by the validator.

runtime_non_conformance.count_rule: Counts must be non-negative integers and must equal the corresponding detail-array length whenever that array is present.
runtime_non_conformance.runtime_identifier_rule: vendor, platform, runtime, model, and client values are open-ended non-empty strings; no vendor or runtime names are hard-coded by the validator.

runtime_conformance_rules.interpretive_rule: patched means the CAM repair exists; confirmed means intended behaviour was observed in a named runtime; non-confirming means the linked failure remains present or recurred in a named runtime. A non-confirming runtime does not invalidate a patch confirmed elsewhere.

lifecycle_rules.ecosystem_status: Describes whether the external failure remains active, recurring, improving, externally resolved, or unknown.
lifecycle_rules.cam_repair_status: repair_status.status describes CAM's internal response and may be repaired while ecosystem_status remains active or recurring.
lifecycle_rules.verification_status: repair_status.verification_status describes whether the repair is unverified, corpus-verified, observed in one or multiple runtimes, regressed, externally unknown, or not applicable.
lifecycle_rules.non_collapse_rule: No one field may be used to imply both CAM repair completion and external ecosystem resolution.

corpus_coverage_rules: Every failure mode must state its assessment against a named corpus repository, ref, commit, and assessment date.
Implemented repair and retrospective coverage are CAM-side states and do not imply external adoption or ecosystem resolution.
Retrospective coverage must identify the pre-existing instrument and relevant sections without claiming the doctrine originated on the VIGIL patch date.
Partial and uncovered classifications must preserve concrete remaining gaps.
A coverage assessment must never be inferred solely from thematic similarity; the cited instrument must materially govern the failure mechanism.

interpretive_provenance_rules.review_history_rule: Append reviews; do not overwrite or retroactively invent earlier reviewer model identities.
interpretive_provenance_rules.human_editor_rule: Human governance editorship and CAM adoption authority must be distinguished from routine AI analytical review.
